name: 3. 고등 관계부사·복합관계사 200
description: 관계부사 when/where/why/how, 복합관계사 whoever/whatever/whenever/wherever/however + 양보절 용법 200개 (고등 수준)

words.w: I remember the day when we met.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 우리가 만난 날을 기억한다. (시간 선행사)

words.w: This is the time when I feel happy.
Spring is the season when flowers bloom.
I recall the moment when he proposed.
1990 is the year when I was born.
There are days when I feel lonely.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 내가 외로움을 느끼는 날이 있다.

words.w: Do you remember the day when we first met?
words.m: [관계부사 when] 우리가 처음 만난 날을 기억하니?

words.w: This is the month when we have exams.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 이달이 우리가 시험 치는 달이다.

words.w: That was the summer when we went to Jeju.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 그때가 우리가 제주에 간 여름이었다.

words.w: I love the moments when we laugh together.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 우리가 함께 웃는 순간을 사랑한다.

words.w: The week when I fell ill was hard.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 내가 아팠던 그 주는 힘들었다.

words.w: The hour when she arrived was late.
There comes a time when you must decide.
I still remember the day when I graduated.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 내가 졸업한 날이 아직도 기억난다.

words.w: This is the age when children learn fast.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 이 나이에 아이들은 빨리 배운다.

words.w: She missed the years when her kids were young.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 그녀는 아이들이 어렸던 시절을 그리워했다.

words.w: There are moments when silence is best.
He recalled the time when he was poor.
I'll never forget the day when we won.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 우리가 이긴 날을 결코 잊지 않을 것이다.

words.w: Winter is the season when it snows.
That was the day when everything changed.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 그날이 모든 것이 바뀐 날이었다.

words.w: She remembers the night when they met.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 그녀는 그들이 만난 밤을 기억한다.

words.w: This is the time when we need unity.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 지금이 우리가 단결해야 할 때이다.

words.w: I recall the year when we moved.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 우리가 이사 간 해가 기억난다.

words.w: There was a time when I believed in magic.
words.m: [관계부사 when] 내가 마법을 믿던 시절이 있었다.

words.w: This is the house where I live.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 이것이 내가 사는 집이다. (장소 선행사)

words.w: The park where we played is gone.
That is the city where I was born.
I know a place where we can talk.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 우리가 이야기할 수 있는 곳을 안다.

words.w: This is the restaurant where we met.
The school where I study is big.
Paris is the city where I want to live.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 파리는 내가 살고 싶은 도시이다.

words.w: The beach where we stayed was clean.
She went back to the town where she grew up.
The room where I study is quiet.
This is the bench where we first kissed.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 여기가 우리가 처음 키스한 벤치이다.

words.w: I miss the country where I was born.
The library where I read is peaceful.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 내가 책 읽는 도서관은 평화롭다.

words.w: That's the cafe where I work.
The mountain where we hiked was steep.
I love the garden where roses bloom.
The theater where we saw the play is closed.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 우리가 연극을 본 극장은 문을 닫았다.

words.w: Find the spot where the X marks.
words.m: [관계부사 where] X 표시가 있는 지점을 찾아라.

words.w: The desk where I write is messy.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 내가 글 쓰는 책상은 엉망이다.

words.w: The office where he works is new.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 그가 일하는 사무실은 새 것이다.

words.w: That is the corner where we waited.
The street where I grew up is quiet.
The ocean is the place where I find peace.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 바다는 내가 평화를 찾는 곳이다.

words.w: This is the bridge where the accident happened.
The hotel where we stayed had a pool.
words.m: [관계부사 where] 우리가 묵은 호텔에는 수영장이 있었다.

words.w: Tell me the reason why you are late.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 왜 늦었는지 이유를 말해 줘. (이유 선행사: the reason)

words.w: I don't know the reason why he left.
This is the reason why I called.
Do you know the reason why she cried?
The reason why he failed is laziness.
I asked her the reason why she quit.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 나는 그녀에게 그만둔 이유를 물었다.

words.w: That is the reason why I love you.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 그것이 내가 너를 사랑하는 이유이다.

words.w: The reason why I'm here is to help.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 내가 여기 있는 이유는 돕기 위함이다.

words.w: There are many reasons why people read.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 사람들이 책을 읽는 많은 이유가 있다.

words.w: Explain the reason why you did this.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 네가 이것을 한 이유를 설명해 달라.

words.w: I understand the reason why she is upset.
Tell me the reason why you chose this.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 네가 이것을 선택한 이유를 말해 줘.

words.w: The reason why I study is to learn.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 내가 공부하는 이유는 배우기 위함이다.

words.w: No one knows the reason why he disappeared.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 그가 사라진 이유를 아무도 모른다.

words.w: That's the reason why I came.
Can you give me a reason why?
words.m: [관계부사 why] 이유를 말해 줄 수 있니?

words.w: She told me the reason why she was angry.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 그녀는 왜 화났는지 이유를 내게 말했다.

words.w: The reason why he won is practice.
I wonder the reason why it happened.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 왜 그런 일이 있었는지 궁금하다.

words.w: This is why I'm worried.
words.m: [관계부사 why] 이것이 내가 걱정하는 이유이다. (the reason 생략 가능)

words.w: This is how I solved it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 이것이 내가 푼 방법이다. (how = the way that)

words.w: Tell me how you did it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 어떻게 했는지 말해 줘.

words.w: That's the way I like it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그것이 내가 좋아하는 방식이다.

words.w: I learned how she cooks.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 나는 그녀가 요리하는 방법을 배웠다.

words.w: Show me how you wrote this.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 네가 이것을 어떻게 썼는지 보여 줘.

words.w: This is how it works.
Explain how you got here.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 어떻게 여기 왔는지 설명해 줘.

words.w: I don't know how he found out.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그가 어떻게 알아냈는지 모른다.

words.w: This is the way to success.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 이것이 성공으로 가는 길이다.

words.w: Look at the way he smiles.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그가 웃는 방식을 봐라.

words.w: I love the way she speaks.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 나는 그녀가 말하는 방식을 좋아한다.

words.w: Tell me the way you feel.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 네가 느끼는 방식을 말해 줘.

words.w: This is how you make it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 이것이 그것을 만드는 방법이다.

words.w: I like the way you think.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 나는 네가 생각하는 방식을 좋아한다.

words.w: Show me how it's done.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그것이 어떻게 되는지 보여 줘.

words.w: That's not how I see it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그건 내가 보는 방식이 아니다.

words.w: This is how we do things here.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 여기서는 이렇게 일을 처리한다.

words.w: I admire the way she handles things.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 나는 그녀가 일을 처리하는 방식에 감탄한다.

words.w: Explain the way this machine works.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 이 기계가 작동하는 방식을 설명해 줘.

words.w: That's how I feel about it.
words.m: [관계부사 how/the way] 그것이 내가 느끼는 방식이다.

words.w: Whoever wants it can have it.
words.m: [whoever] 원하는 사람은 누구든 가질 수 있다. (= anyone who)

words.w: Whoever comes first will get a prize.
words.m: [whoever] 먼저 오는 사람은 누구든 상을 받을 것이다.

words.w: Whoever you are, welcome.
Give it to whoever wants it.
Whoever breaks the rule will be punished.
words.m: [whoever] 규칙을 어긴 사람은 누구든 처벌될 것이다.

words.w: Whoever arrives first wins.
I'll help whoever needs help.
words.m: [whoever] 도움이 필요한 사람은 누구든 도울 것이다.

words.w: Whoever did this, please come forward.
Whoever told you that was wrong.
words.m: [whoever] 네게 그런 말 한 사람이 누구든 잘못됐다.

words.w: Whoever tries hardest will succeed.
words.m: [whoever] 가장 열심히 하는 사람이 성공할 것이다.

words.w: You can bring whoever you want.
Whoever knocks first must come in.
Whoever steals will be caught.
Whoever sings best gets the role.
words.m: [whoever] 노래를 가장 잘 부르는 사람이 그 역할을 얻는다.

words.w: Thank whoever helped you.
Whoever forgets will lose points.
Invite whoever you like.
Whoever doubts this is wrong.
Whoever tried it liked it.
Whoever has the ticket can enter.
words.m: [whoever] 표가 있는 사람은 누구든 들어갈 수 있다.

words.w: Whatever happens, I'll be there.
words.m: [whatever] 무슨 일이 있어도 내가 있을 것이다. (= anything that)

words.w: Do whatever you want.
Whatever he says, don't believe him.
Eat whatever you like.
Whatever you do, do it well.
I'll buy whatever you need.
words.m: [whatever] 네가 필요한 것은 뭐든 사 줄게.

words.w: Whatever it is, I'll help.
Whatever you decide is fine.
Whatever happens, stay calm.
Say whatever is on your mind.
Whatever he touches turns to gold.
words.m: [whatever] 그가 만지는 것은 뭐든 금이 된다.

words.w: Choose whatever you want.
Whatever you say, I'll listen.
Take whatever you need.
Whatever the reason, I forgive you.
Whatever she does, she does well.
Ask whatever you want.
Whatever you plan, I'll support.
Whatever made you think so?
words.m: [whatever] 도대체 뭐가 너를 그렇게 생각하게 만들었니? (의문사 강조)

words.w: You can wear whatever you like.
words.m: [whatever] 원하는 것 뭐든 입을 수 있다.

words.w: Whatever may come, I will face it.
Tell me whatever you remember.
words.m: [whatever] 네가 기억하는 것 뭐든 말해 줘.

words.w: She agrees to whatever he proposes.
words.m: [whatever] 그녀는 그가 제안하는 것 뭐든 동의한다.

words.w: Whatever you did, thanks.
Whatever the cost, we'll buy it.
words.m: [whatever] 가격이 얼마든 우리는 그것을 살 것이다.

words.w: Come whenever you want.
Whenever I see her, I smile.
words.m: [whenever] 그녀를 볼 때마다 나는 미소 짓는다.

words.w: Call me whenever you need.
Whenever it rains, I feel sad.
She complains whenever she's tired.
Whenever you're ready, let's go.
I listen to music whenever I study.
Whenever he speaks, everyone listens.
Come over whenever you can.
words.m: [whenever] 올 수 있을 때 언제든 와라.

words.w: Whenever she calls, I answer.
I get nervous whenever I speak.
Whenever we meet, we laugh.
Call whenever it's urgent.
Whenever I travel, I learn new things.
words.m: [whenever] 여행할 때마다 나는 새로운 것을 배운다.

words.w: He helps me whenever I ask.
Whenever you like, come visit.
Whenever it snows, kids are happy.
I cry whenever I hear that song.
words.m: [whenever] 그 노래를 들을 때마다 나는 운다.

words.w: Whenever she enters, the room brightens.
Stop by whenever you want.
Go wherever you want.
I'll follow wherever you lead.
Wherever she goes, trouble follows.
You can sit wherever.
Wherever I go, I remember you.
He feels at home wherever he is.
words.m: [wherever] 그는 있는 곳 어디든 편하게 느낀다.

words.w: Put it wherever you like.
Wherever we traveled, people smiled.
words.m: [wherever] 우리가 여행한 어디든 사람들이 미소 지었다.

words.w: She follows him wherever he goes.
I'll meet you wherever you say.
Wherever there is life, there is hope.
words.m: [wherever] 삶이 있는 곳 어디든 희망이 있다.

words.w: Sit wherever you feel comfortable.
I'll be with you wherever you are.
Wherever we looked, there was beauty.
Wherever you want to go, we'll go.
words.m: [wherever] 네가 가고 싶은 곳 어디든 갈게.

words.w: However hard you try, you may fail.
words.m: [however] 아무리 열심히 해도 실패할 수 있다. (however + 형용사/부사)

words.w: However rich he is, he is unhappy.
However you do it, be careful.
However it happens, I'll help.
However tired I am, I keep working.
However long it takes, I'll wait.
However cold it gets, he exercises.
However fast you run, she runs faster.
words.m: [however] 네가 아무리 빨라도 그녀가 더 빠르다.

words.w: However small the room, I love it.
However difficult, I will try.
However you feel, talk to me.
However late it is, call me.
However poor he is, he is honest.
However loud he shouts, no one hears.
words.m: [however] 그가 아무리 크게 소리쳐도 아무도 듣지 않는다.

words.w: However much it costs, buy it.
Whoever may come, I don't care.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 누가 오든 상관없다. (= No matter who)

words.w: Whatever happens, we must continue.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 무슨 일이 있어도 계속해야 한다. (= No matter what)

words.w: Wherever you go, I'll find you.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 네가 어디로 가든 찾을 것이다. (= No matter where)

words.w: Whenever he arrives, let him in.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 그가 언제 도착하든 들여라. (= No matter when)

words.w: However you decide, I'll support.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 네가 어떻게 결정하든 지원할 것이다. (= No matter how)

words.w: Whichever you choose, it's fine.
Whoever she is, treat her with respect.
Whatever you say, I won't change.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 네가 뭐라고 해도 나는 바뀌지 않을 것이다.

words.w: However high the price, I'll pay.
Whenever you need me, I'm here.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 네가 언제 필요하든 나는 여기 있다.

words.w: Wherever they hide, we'll find them.
words.m: [복합관계사-양보] 그들이 어디 숨든 우리는 찾을 것이다.

words.w: Whichever route we take, it's long.
However hard the task, we'll finish.
Whoever called, I didn't answer.
Whatever the weather, we'll go.
